name: Wayne County Homeless Action Network (WCHNA) Emergency Shelter
eligibility: Wayne County residents experiencing homelessness; youth ages 18–24 prioritized
intake_process: Walk-in accepted; call ahead for same-day placement. Bring valid ID if available.

name: Covenant House Michigan
address: 2959 Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Detroit, MI 48208
eligibility: Youth ages 18–24 experiencing homelessness or housing instability
intake_process: Walk in any time to the crisis shelter. Case managers available daily for transitional housing assessment.

name: Gleaners Community Food Bank – Detroit Distribution
hours: Mon–Fri 8am–4pm; distribution events vary by location
eligibility: Any Wayne County resident; no income verification required at distributions
intake_process: Find a food distribution near you at gcfb.org/find-food. No registration required for most distributions.

name: Detroit Central City Community Mental Health
eligibility: Wayne County residents; Medicaid, Medicare, and sliding-scale fees accepted
intake_process: Call to schedule an intake appointment. Crisis walk-ins accepted. Medicaid enrollment assistance available.

name: Michigan Legal Aid – Detroit Office
address: 611 W Ottawa St, Lansing, MI 48933 (serving Wayne County remotely)
eligibility: Low-income individuals; income guidelines apply (generally 125% federal poverty level)
intake_process: Call or apply online at MichiganLegalHelp.org. Phone intake available. No walk-ins.

name: National Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
eligibility: Anyone in emotional distress or suicidal crisis
intake_process: Call or text 988. Chat available at 988lifeline.org. No registration required.

name: Monroe County Food Bank
eligibility: Monroe County residents; proof of address helpful but not always required
intake_process: Visit during open hours. First visit requires a brief intake form. Bring proof of Monroe County address if available.

name: Monroe County Community Health & Social Services
eligibility: Monroe County residents; many programs income-based
intake_process: Call or walk in. Bring ID, proof of income, and proof of Monroe County residency. Benefits enrollment assistance available.

name: Transition to Independence (TTI) Youth Program
eligibility: Young adults ages 16–24 exiting foster care, juvenile justice, or experiencing housing instability
intake_process: Call to schedule intake or drop in during business hours. Case manager assigned within 48 hours.

name: Detroit Rescue Mission Ministries – Crisis Center
eligibility: Wayne County adults and youth in crisis; no documentation required for emergency intake
intake_process: Walk in at any time for emergency shelter and meals. No ID required for initial placement.

name: Wayne Metro Community Action Agency – Utility & Housing Assistance
eligibility: Low-income Wayne County households; income at or below 200% federal poverty level
intake_process: Call to schedule an appointment. Bring ID, proof of income, utility bills, and lease/mortgage statement.

name: Monroe County Legal Self-Help Center
eligibility: Anyone representing themselves in Monroe County courts
intake_process: Walk in during business hours. Staff can help with forms but cannot provide legal advice. Free of charge.
